name: Lewis & Clark Bank
description_short: Maps Credit Union (formerly Lewis & Clark Bank) is a member-owned financial cooperative serving the Mid-Willamette Valley of Oregon with 90 years of history, offering personal and business banking, lending, and wealth management services.
description_long:
Maps Credit Union operates as a federally-chartered credit union serving the Mid-Willamette Valley region of Oregon. The organization has a 90-year history of community banking, having evolved from its Lewis & Clark Bank origins. As a credit union, Maps operates on a member-ownership model rather than shareholder profit maximization, positioning itself around cooperative financial principles and community reinvestment.

Maps Credit Union offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, and digital banking tools. On the lending side, they provide personal loans, auto/RV financing, home mortgages, home equity loans, and business credit products. The organization also provides wealth management services through a Private Client division, investment services, insurance products (Maps Insurance), and loan protection offerings. Their digital offerings include mobile banking, mobile check deposit, mobile wallet, and various financial calculators.

The credit union distinguishes itself through community commitment initiatives including the Maps Community Foundation, community grants, education scholarships, teacher grants, school supply grants, and Individual Development Accounts (IDAs). They offer specialized programs for school employees, youth savings programs, and free credit coaching. The organization emphasizes security with dedicated security blogs and scam alerts, and provides employment and tenant screening services for business members. They also accommodate cannabis business accounts, reflecting regional economic realities.

As a regional credit union, Maps' reach is geographically limited to Oregon members, which may restrict accessibility for those outside the Mid-Willamette Valley. While they offer competitive products across banking, lending, and investments, their scale is smaller than national banks. The website content suggests strong community focus but limited detail on specific rates, fees, and product terms compared to larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal loans and lines of credit
Business credit cards and lines of credit
pros: Credit union member-ownership model typically results in better rates and lower fees compared to commercial banks
Comprehensive financial services under one institution: banking, lending, wealth management, and insurance
Strong community commitment with dedicated foundation, scholarships, grants, and Individual Development Accounts
Specialized offerings including free credit coaching and cannabis business accounts (rare in traditional banking)
90-year established history with deep regional roots in Mid-Willamette Valley
Digital banking tools including mobile wallet, mobile check deposit, and online account access
Business lending services including SBA-style loans, equipment financing, and commercial lines of credit
cons: Geographic limitation: membership restricted to Mid-Willamette Valley region of Oregon, excluding national access
Website lacks specific interest rates, APY figures, and fee schedules beyond limited featured rates
Limited transparency on loan approval requirements, credit score minimums, or qualification criteria
Smaller institution scale may mean fewer branch locations and ATM access compared to national banks
No indication of CDFI certification or lending to underserved populations beyond stated community programs
best_for: Mid-Willamette Valley Oregon residents seeking full-service banking with community focus and member-owned governance
Small business owners in the region needing comprehensive commercial banking and specialized services like cannabis accounts
Educators and school employees benefiting from specialized programs and employee discounts
Consumers prioritizing credit union philosophy and community reinvestment over corporate banking structures
diagnosis: Maps Credit Union is best for Oregon residents in the Mid-Willamette Valley seeking comprehensive, member-owned banking with strong community values and competitive rates. The main caveat is strict geographic membership limitation, making this institution inaccessible to consumers outside their service region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
